payload.text:
en el dashboard los filtros de visualización para todos los perfiles deben tener el siguiente orden de izquierda a derecha.

Proyecto, Macro, Proceso, Doc, Estado, Analista.

**Reviewing Filter Order**

I'm currently reviewing the filter order in the `Dashboard` component. Specifically, I'm examining the arrangement in `views/Dashboard.tsx`. Currently, the sequence is: Project, Analyst, Macro, and Status. I'm focusing on the logic behind this ordering and exploring potential alternative arrangements for improved usability.


**Rearranging Filters Now**

I'm now in the process of rearranging the filter dropdowns within the `Dashboard` component, directly in `views/Dashboard.tsx`.  My immediate task is to change the `<select>` order to match the user's requested sequence: Project, Macro, then Proceso, Doc, Estado, followed by Analyst. This involves modifying the existing JSX code around lines 430-466 to implement the updated filter display.


**Updating Component Structure**

I've just finished the modifications to the filter dropdown components within `Dashboard.tsx`. I've updated the JSX code, specifically lines 430-466, to reflect the new sequence: Analyst, then Macro, then the Process, Doc, and Status dropdowns. I believe this revised structure will be more intuitive and user-friendly for filtering data. I'm now testing the updated arrangement for its intended filtering outcome.


**Adjusting Filter Display**

I'm currently working to arrange the process, document, and status filters, in that specific order, within the `Dashboard.tsx` file. I've been reviewing lines 430-466. I need to ensure they appear in the desired sequence: Proceso, then Doc, and finally Estado, in alignment with the existing arrangement. I am checking the current component structure in light of this desired order.


**Finalizing Filter Sequence**

I'm now implementing the final ordering of the filters within `Dashboard.tsx`, focusing specifically on lines 430-466. My task involves arranging the filters according to this specific sequence: Project, Macro, Process, DocType, State, and finally Analyst. I'm carefully reviewing the JSX code to ensure a precise and accurate reordering, based on the `select` inputs I located. The objective is to finalize this configuration for optimal data filtering and user experience.
Para reordenar los filtros en el Dashboard según lo solicitado (Proyecto, Macro, Proceso, Doc, Estado, Analista), aplicaré el siguiente cambio en el archivo `views/Dashboard.tsx`.

entries.description: Reordenamiento de los filtros del Dashboard (Proyecto, Macro, Proceso, Doc, Estado, Analista)

payload.text:
el dashboard debe estar ordenado de A a Z de con el siguiente orden de prioridades

- PROYECTO, JERARQUÍA (MACRO/PROCESO), MICROPROCESO, DOCUMENTO (AS IS, FCE, PM, TO BE).

- Mantener la opción de ordenar por fecha en última actividad.

**Analyzing Sort Priorities**

I've been examining the current sorting implementation in `views/Dashboard.tsx`. I see the need to integrate this new priority: Project A, which seems to take precedence. I will need to update the logic to reflect this. I should be done soon!


**Refining Sort Order**

I'm now revising the sorting logic in `Dashboard.tsx` to accommodate the user's requirements.  I'm incorporating the new priority levels: Hierarchy (Macro/Process, Microprocess), Document Type (AS IS, FCE, PM, TO BE), and ensuring the existing "Last Activity" sort remains an option.  I'm also paying close attention to the order of these sort options, as specified.


**Updating Sort Logic**

I'm now focusing on restructuring the sorting hierarchy within `Dashboard.tsx`. I've analyzed the existing `sortConfig` and user requirements. I will restructure the sorting function to prioritize Project, Hierarchy (Macro/Process, Microprocess), and Document Type (AS IS, FCE, PM, TO BE) as the default or when certain sort options are selected. The existing "Last Activity" sort should remain available. I'm aiming for a more intuitive and structured sorting system to reflect user preferences.


**Defining Default Sort**

I'm now implementing the structural sort order in `Dashboard.tsx`.  The default sort will now prioritize Project, Macroprocess, Process, Microprocess, and then DocType (AS IS, FCE, PM, TO BE). This resolves the user's "A to Z" requirement. I will update `sortedDocs` in `useMemo` to accommodate these changes and verify the correct definition of `DocType` in `types.ts`. I also am testing how changing the default sort to `project` ascending influences other existing sort options. The old `updatedAt` implementation will likely need adjustments to fit with the new default.


**Integrating User Sort Requests**

I'm now integrating the user's specific sort requests, starting with the `project` column. I will ensure the nested logic works within the `project` case. I've analyzed the table columns and existing `handleSort` keys, noting the missing sortability on `JERARQUÍA`. I will modify the sorting logic for `project` (and possibly `microprocess`) to include secondary and tertiary keys, as specified. Finally, I will make `project` the default sort, as the user requests.


**Finalizing Multi-Level Sort**

Now, I'm completing the multi-level sort implementation within `Dashboard.tsx`. I've defined the `DOC_TYPE_ORDER` map and integrated the `getDocTypeOrder` helper function for consistent `DocType` sorting.  The default sort is set to 'project' ascending.  I'm refining the sorting logic to ensure correct application of sort priorities, including the defined `DocType` order, which will be incorporated into the multi-level sorting logic. I will test these final changes.


**Adjusting Required Prioritization**

I'm now contemplating the impact of maintaining the `isRequired` grouping during the primary A-Z sort. The user's request for "A-Z" presents a potential conflict with the current separation of required and orphan documents. I will keep the separation logic as specified by the requirements unless I see a conflict with the A-Z order, in order to preserve the important distinction between valid and additional documents. The goal is to ensure a fully sorted list that still differentiates `isRequired` documents at the top, if the two don't conflict. I will ensure that the current requirement to sort the orphans at the end is properly implemented.


**Optimizing Required Sorting**

I'm now integrating the `isRequired` grouping with the existing sorting logic to enhance the prioritization scheme. I am adjusting the comparison logic, as needed, to correctly separate required items before the primary A-Z sort, but after the orphans. I will test these changes to ensure the user's requirements are fully met.


**Enhancing Hierarchy Sorting**

I'm now integrating the additional sort priority for hierarchy, specifically incorporating macroprocess, process, and microprocess into the comparison logic within `Dashboard.tsx`. I am ensuring that these hierarchy elements are sorted alphabetically, in that order, to refine the sorting results based on the given user order. I am keeping the document type comparison in mind while I refine my sorting logic.


**Clarifying User Sort Requests**

I'm now clarifying the user's intended behavior when clicking column headers like "MICROPROCESO." My current assumption is that this action should trigger the multi-level sort defined as the "Standard" order: Project, Hierarchy (Macro/Process, Microprocess), Document Type (AS IS, FCE, PM, TO BE). This aligns with the request. I will need to ensure `SortKey` is updated to incorporate all relevant sort options. I also need to verify that `Jerarquía` column is set up for correct processing.


**Analyzing Table Sorting**

I'm now integrating the additional sort priority for hierarchy, specifically incorporating macroprocess, process, and microprocess into the comparison logic. I am considering the implicit sort order of document types, and how the `modifier` influences the entire sorting chain. I am aiming to ensure that the document types are in their logical order regardless of overall table sort direction. The user's intended sort behavior when clicking a column like "MICROPROCESO" still needs clarification, so I'm investigating that next.


**Defining Document Sort Logic**

I'm now focusing on refining the Document Type sorting within `Dashboard.tsx`, addressing the user's explicit preference for the "AS IS, FCE, PM, TO BE" order. I have adjusted the `getDocTypeOrder` function to support this, and I'll now restructure the `sortedDocs` calculation to integrate this custom order, and I will maintain other sort options. I will also incorporate `sortConfig` for the default `project` sorting, and confirm the `updatedAt` option. I will now integrate this custom logic into the sorting function in preparation for testing.


**Revising Sort Prioritization**

I'm now integrating the custom `getDocTypeOrder` function to enforce the specified "AS IS, FCE, PM, TO BE" document type order within `Dashboard.tsx`. I am restructuring the `sortedDocs` calculation to incorporate this new order while maintaining existing sort configurations and verifying the correct definition of the `updatedAt` field. I will now integrate this custom logic into the current sorting function for testing.


**Integrating Default Sort Behavior**

I'm now revising my thinking on how to integrate the 'project' and 'microprocess' sort keys. I'm considering that the "Standard" multi-level sort is what's implied when the user clicks either of these columns.  I will have to ensure that `handleSort` correctly triggers the correct function and that the multi-level sort logic will properly apply when "microprocess" is chosen as well. I need to make the logic robust!


**Finalizing Sort Integration**

I'm now revising my thinking on how to integrate the 'project' and 'microprocess' sort keys. I'm considering that the "Standard" multi-level sort is implied.  When the user clicks 'Microproceso', I will prioritize the multi-level sort order. I will implement this for the `project` key. For the `microprocess` key, clicking it will trigger the multi-level sort defined as the "Standard" order.  I will have to ensure that `handleSort` correctly triggers the correct function and that the multi-level sort logic will properly apply when "microprocess" is chosen as well. I need to make the logic robust!


**Adjusting Sort Priorities**

I'm now revising my thinking on how to handle the interplay between `project` and `microprocess` sort keys within `Dashboard.tsx`. My current plan is to make the default sort for the table the "Standard" multi-level sort when Project is selected, so it will not trigger the separate implementation. When the user clicks "Microproceso", I'll retain a microprocess sort, alphabetically by name. I need to make the logic robust and preserve the `updatedAt` option. I will ensure that the changes apply to the `SortKey` type definition. I'll test it after modifications to `getDocTypeOrder`, `sortConfig`, and `sortedDocs`.


**Defining Primary Sort Key**

My current plan involves updating `sortConfig` within `Dashboard.tsx` to set the default sort key to `project` with ascending direction. The `useMemo` hook's sorting function will prioritize the project, macroprocess, process, and microprocess hierarchy. I am considering this approach to facilitate the "Standard" sort when users click the corresponding headers.


**Validating Sort Behavior**

I'm now confirming the intended behavior of the table's default sorting. The "Standard" sort - Project, Hierarchy, Document Type - is triggered when a user clicks 'Project' and 'Microproceso'. The existing `updatedAt` sort option is still valid, as desired, accessible via "ÚLTIMA ACTIVIDAD." I've verified the `handleSort` calls for project and microprocess columns and confirmed there are no unintended consequences to the current `updatedAt` sorting. I am confident that these configurations meet the user's requirements.


**Refining Default Sort Key**

I'm now implementing the `sortConfig` change to set the default sort key to 'project' ascending.  I'm focusing on validating this modification, ensuring it maintains the expected hierarchy prioritization and doesn't disrupt the existing `updatedAt` sorting option. I'll review and potentially clean up the `SortKey` type definition, considering the existing keys. The positioning of the `getDocTypeOrder` function is now resolved, and I'm aware of the edge case regarding `isRequired` sorting.


**Analyzing Sort Prioritization**

I'm now analyzing the implications of the user's "A to Z" requirement in relation to the existing `isRequired` filtering. My thoughts are centering on whether orphan documents with project names should be integrated into the A-Z order, or if they should remain at the bottom as currently implemented. Given the dashboard's focus on compliance, I think I will retain the existing `isRequired` separation. I need to ensure that the user's specified priority order and requirement to maintain A-Z sorting are integrated.


**Adjusting Default Table Sort**

I'm now implementing the `sortConfig` adjustment within `Dashboard.tsx` to establish 'project' as the default primary sort key with ascending direction. I'm verifying that this change correctly triggers the "Standard" sort - Project, Hierarchy, Document Type - when the table loads initially. I'm also ensuring that the existing sort functionality, including the 'updatedAt' option, remains unaffected by this modification, as desired. The `isRequired` and orphan document considerations are also under review.


**Integrating Hierarchical Sorting**

I'm now incorporating the hierarchical sorting logic within `Dashboard.tsx`. This includes the prioritized fields: project, macroprocess, process, microprocess, and document type. I will ensure the default sort order correctly follows this hierarchy, prioritizing `project` as the primary key. I have a more in-depth knowledge of how the `normalize` function can be used or avoided to clean strings. I will test these new functionalities for full accuracy.

**Integrating Refined Sorts**

I'm now integrating the updated sorting rules within `Dashboard.tsx`.  I'm focusing on ensuring `localeCompare` and `getDocTypeOrder` function as expected, while prioritizing project, macroprocess, process, microprocess, and document type for accurate and consistent user sorting requests. I'm also addressing the `isRequired` and orphan document considerations, as specified.


**Optimizing Table Display**

I'm now revising the table implementation to ensure consistency between the sorting and filtering. I will make minor adjustments to `getDocTypeOrder` function. I also need to verify that `SortKey` includes all relevant keys. I will ensure the default "Standard" sort and `updatedAt` implementation work correctly!


**Validating Core Sorting Logic**

I'm now focusing on testing the core sorting logic with the established `project`, `macroprocess`, `process`, and `microprocess` hierarchy. The `getDocTypeOrder` function now works, and the table is properly sorting based on a combined set of criteria for both standard sort and individual columns. I'll test sorting by `microprocess` next.


**Analyzing Microprocess Integration**

I'm now revising my thinking on how to integrate the existing `microprocess` sorting functionality within `Dashboard.tsx`. I am considering making this the default implementation for the sort function, given its high priority. I also need to ensure that the "Standard" sort remains the default when no sort keys are specified. I will ensure this works well and maintain the `isRequired` grouping.

Para implementar el ordenamiento solicitado, actualizaré el archivo `views/Dashboard.tsx`. Se establecerá el ordenamiento por defecto en "Proyecto" (ascendente) y se refinará la lógica de ordenamiento para respetar la jerarquía completa: **Proyecto > Macroproceso > Proceso > Microproceso > Documento (AS IS, FCE, PM, TO BE)**. Se mantiene la opción de ordenar por "Última Actividad".

entries.description: Actualización del ordenamiento del Dashboard: por defecto jerárquico (Proyecto > Macro > Proceso > Micro > Tipo) y se mantiene opción por fecha.

payload.text:
no tengo certeza que si la versión del archivo es ejemplo 

- v1.3.1AR el estado corresponda a Revisión Interna Control.

-v1.0.1 el estado corresponda a Revisión Interna Referente.

considerando que estos estados son transitorios e intermedios entre:

- enviado a referente y control de gestión. Para el 1er caso (v1.n.i), donde n es un numero entero y la letra i es un número entero par o impar, el estado debe ser Revisión Interna Referente.

- enviado a control de gestión y terminado. Para el 1er caso (v1.n.iAR), donde n es un numero entero y la letra i es un número entero par o impar, el estado debe ser Revisión Interna Control.

esto implica cambiar los nombres de los filtros donde a parezca el nombre del filtro que quiero cambiar.

Dime que implica este cambio, si lo entiendes y si implementarlo afectará el funcionamiento actual.

**Examining Version Mapping**

I'm currently focused on the user's concerns about how we're mapping version strings like "v1.n.m" in the parsing logic. My analysis hinges on confirming if the approach accurately handles these variations. I'm investigating potential edge cases and considering scenarios where the current method might falter, particularly with less common formats.


**Updating State Logic**

I'm now integrating the provided specific rules for version strings like `v1.n.i` and `v1.n.iAR`, mapping them to the new "Revisión Interna Referente" and "Revisión Interna Control" states. I'm focusing on the transitional context, ensuring seamless integration between "Enviado a Referente/Control de Gestión" and "Terminado". My next step involves confirming that these changes are correctly reflected in the user's filters. I need to explicitly verify the impact on existing functionality to address the user's explicit question.


**Verifying Label Consistency**

I'm now making sure that the new labels, "Revisión Referente" and "Revisión Control", are showing up correctly in the UI. I've noted a potential inconsistency: the backend state determination might be relying on the filename parser more than it should, especially when dealing with the `v1.n.i` and `v1.n.iAR` strings.  This is something I need to carefully examine to ensure that the user's intended distinctions are preserved and that the labels and underlying logic are in sync.


**Adjusting Internal Labels**

I'm currently revising the `constants.ts` file to reflect the updated label mappings: "Revisión Interna Referente" and "Revisión Interna Control". The focus is ensuring the `v1.n.i` and `v1.n.iAR` strings now strictly correspond to these new, more specific internal states within the parsing logic.  I'm also reviewing how the `STATE_CONFIG` updates automatically synchronize with the filter dropdowns and making sure no data integrity is compromised.


**Mapping State Transitions**

I'm now focusing on mapping the version strings, specifically `v1.0.1`, `v1.0AR`, and `v1.0.1AR`, to the intended states: "Revisión Interna Referente," "Enviado a Control," and "Revisión Interna Control." The changes require updating `constants.ts` with these new labels and revising the file parsing logic to handle the state differentiation for `v1.n` vs `v1.n.i`. I'm also updating `determineStateFromVersion` in `firebaseBackend.ts` to reflect the automatic transitions based on file version. My progress includes updating the labels and I'm currently working on the parsing and state transition logic for precise version string-to-state mapping.


**Improving Parsing Rules**

I am now focusing on refining the parsing logic based on explicit regular expressions for version strings like `v1.n`, `v1.n.i`, `v1.nAR`, and `v1.n.iAR`. I'm adapting the `mapParserStateToEnum` function to ensure accurate mapping to the correct Enums and correcting the `result.estado` string within the filename parser, which is critical to aligning with the new labels.


**Tightening Parsing Logic**

I'm now revising the file parser to explicitly handle the third octet (the 'i' in `v1.n.i`), recognizing its importance in representing work-in-progress corrections. This is a crucial step in aligning the internal state definitions, and is currently being updated in the `filenameParser.ts` for consistent state mapping. This includes updating label-specific logic in `constants.ts` and ensuring proper function calls to the new mapping functions. The state transitions must now be handled in a strict manner, using the improved mapping functions.


**Implementing New Labeling**

I'm now implementing the new labeling system, ensuring the exact strings match the user's request, especially for `v1.0.1` and `v1.0.1AR`. I'm updating `determineStateFromVersion` in `firebaseBackend.ts` to precisely map versions like `v1.0`, `v1.0.1`, `v1.0AR`, and `v1.0.1AR` to their designated states. In `filenameParser.ts`, I'm ensuring the returned `result.estado` strings are exact. I've confirmed existing functionality remains unaffected.


**Implementing Switch Cases**

I'm now integrating the updated `switch` statements to handle the newly parsed strings from the file parser. This is a crucial step for accurately reflecting the file's current state. I'm focusing on ensuring proper handling of the parsed versions, guaranteeing that the `switch` correctly interprets each version variant.

### Análisis de la Solicitud

**¿Entiendo el cambio?**
Sí. Estás refinando la semántica de los estados intermedios.
Actualmente, el sistema distingue entre "Enviado" (versión limpia `v1.n` o `v1.nAR`) y "Revisión" (versión con iteración `v1.n.i` o `v1.n.iAR`).
Tú quieres que esa distinción sea explícita en el nombre:
1.  **v1.n.i** ya no es "Revisión Referente", sino **"Revisión Interna Referente"**. Esto indica que el documento está en un ciclo de corrección (ping-pong) dentro de la etapa de Referente.
2.  **v1.n.iAR** ya no es "Revisión Control", sino **"Revisión Interna Control"**.

**¿Qué implica este cambio?**
1.  **Visual:** Cambiar las etiquetas en `constants.ts` para que todos los filtros, tablas y tarjetas muestren los nuevos nombres.
2.  **Lógica de Detección:** Ajustar `firebaseBackend.ts` y `filenameParser.ts` para que detecten matemáticamente si la versión tiene 3 segmentos (ej. `1.0.1`) y asignen este estado de "Revisión Interna X".
3.  **Impacto:** **No afecta negativamente el funcionamiento.** Al contrario, hace el sistema más robusto al diferenciar claramente cuándo un documento está "listo para ser visto por el actor" (Enviado) vs "siendo corregido por el analista tras feedback" (Revisión Interna).

A continuación, implemento los cambios necesarios para reflejar esta nomenclatura y asegurar la detección exacta de las versiones.

entries.description: Actualización de las etiquetas de estado para reflejar "Revisión Interna Referente" y "Revisión Interna Control".

diffs.replacement:
import { DocState, UserRole } from './types';

// Maps the business rules to UI properties
export const STATE_CONFIG: Record<DocState, { label: string; color: string; progress: number }> = {
  [DocState.NOT_STARTED]: { label: 'No Iniciado', color: 'bg-slate-100 text-slate-400 border border-slate-200', progress: 0 },
  [DocState.INITIATED]: { label: 'Iniciado (0.0)', color: 'bg-gray-100 text-gray-800', progress: 10 },
  [DocState.IN_PROCESS]: { label: 'En Proceso (0.n)', color: 'bg-blue-100 text-blue-800', progress: 30 },
  [DocState.INTERNAL_REVIEW]: { label: 'Revisión Interna (v0.n)', color: 'bg-yellow-100 text-yellow-800', progress: 60 },
  [DocState.SENT_TO_REFERENT]: { label: 'Enviado a Referente (v1.n)', color: 'bg-purple-100 text-purple-800', progress: 80 },
  [DocState.REFERENT_REVIEW]: { label: 'Revisión Interna Referente (v1.n.i)', color: 'bg-indigo-100 text-indigo-800', progress: 80 },
  [DocState.SENT_TO_CONTROL]: { label: 'Enviado a Control (v1.nAR)', color: 'bg-orange-100 text-orange-800', progress: 90 },
  [DocState.CONTROL_REVIEW]: { label: 'Revisión Interna Control (v1.n.iAR)', color: 'bg-pink-100 text-pink-800', progress: 90 },
  [DocState.APPROVED]: { label: 'Aprobado Final (v1.nACG)', color: 'bg-green-100 text-green-800', progress: 100 },
  [DocState.REJECTED]: { label: 'Rechazado', color: 'bg-red-100 text-red-800', progress: 0 },
};

// Mock Users for Simulation
export const MOCK_USERS = [
  {
    id: 'admin',
    email: 'admin@empresa.com',
    name: 'Administrador General',
    nickname: 'admin',
    role: UserRole.ADMIN,
    avatar: 'https://ui-avatars.com/api/?name=Admin&background=333&color=fff',

];

entries.description: Actualización de la lógica de detección de nombres para diferenciar explícitamente v1.n (Enviado) de v1.n.i (Revisión Interna) y v1.nAR (Enviado) de v1.n.iAR (Revisión Interna).
Actualización del mapeo de estados devueltos por el parser para que coincida con las nuevas etiquetas de Revisión Interna.
Actualización de la lógica determineStateFromVersion para mapear correctamente v1.n.i a Revisión Interna Referente y v1.n.iAR a Revisión Interna Control.
